content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/Mappings/components/PlanMappingsActionsBar/PlanMappingsActionsBar.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 34, 45
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 34):
   33            <Button
   34              variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
   35              onClick={onUpdate}

  (line 45):
   44            <Button
   45              variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
   46              onClick={() => {

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 33, 44
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 33):
   32          <FlexItem>
   33            <Button
   34              variant={ButtonVariant.primary}

  (line 44):
   43          <FlexItem>
   44            <Button
   45              variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}

```

### Fix 3
Line: 56
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   55        <HelperText className="forklift-plan-mappings-actions-bar__helper-text">
   56          <HelperTextItem variant="indeterminate">
   57            {t(

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/plans/details/tabs/Mappings/components/PlanMappingsActionsBar/PlanMappingsActionsBar.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1: `Button.variant` type changed
Lines 34 and 45 use `ButtonVariant.primary` and `ButtonVariant.secondary`. The type change adds `'stateful'` to the union but doesn't remove any values. Both `primary` and `secondary` are still valid values. **No code change needed** — the existing values are still in the new type.

### Fix 2: Icon children to `icon` prop on `<Button>`
Lines 33 and 44 — the `<Button>` components at these lines. Looking at the actual content:
- Line 33-41: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.primary} ...>{t('Update mappings')}</Button>` — contains only text, **no icon**. No change needed.
- Line 44-52: `<Button variant={ButtonVariant.secondary} ...>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — contains only text, **no icon**. No change needed.

### Fix 3: Icon children to `icon` prop on `<HelperTextItem>`
Line 56: `<HelperTextItem variant="indeterminate">` — looking at the content, it contains only text (`{t(...)}`), **no icon**. No change needed.

All three fixes target patterns that don't actually manifest in this file — the Buttons have no icon children, and the HelperTextItem has no icon children. The variant values `primary` and `secondary` remain valid in the new type union.

No changes are necessary to this file.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The values `ButtonVariant.primary` (line 34) and `ButtonVariant.secondary` (line 45) are both still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **No change needed.** Both `<Button>` instances (lines 33–41 and 44–52) contain only text children (`{t('Update mappings')}` and `{t('Cancel')}`), with no icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The migration rule only applies when an icon is present as a child.

3. **Fix 3** (icon children to `icon` prop on HelperTextItem): **No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` at line 56 contains only text children (`{t(...)}`), with no icon elements. The migration rule only applies when an icon is present as a child.
